heroHeadline: Somalia: Opposition Threatens Parallel Election as May 15 Deadline Approaches; EU Approves €75M for AUSSOM; Adan Yabal Counter-Offensive Stalls Day 5
heroSubtitle: Somalia's opposition coalition escalated its constitutional confrontation on April 24, threatening to organize a parallel presidential election 'within one month' if President Mohamud — whose term expires May 15 (21 days away) — refuses inclusive talks. The Somali Future Council called an emergency 'Salvation Summit,' while Puntland and Jubbaland signal readiness to constitute an alternative executive if negotiations fail. In a critical parallel development, the EU approved €75 million for AUSSOM on April 23 under the European Peace Facility — the largest single EU contribution, bringing cumulative EU support to ~€2.8 billion since 2007 — partially offsetting the chronic underfunding that has limited AUSSOM's capacity. On the security front, the SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19 after killing 8 civilians) remains stalled in mobilization on Day 5, as Al-Shabaab consolidates defenses and AFRICOM's air campaign tracks toward 58+ strikes for 2026. The convergence of political vacuum, active multi-front insurgency, and 6.5 million Somalis facing acute hunger represents Somalia's most dangerous crisis since 2021.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, EU EPF, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
